concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a clear agency logo/seal for the North Carolina Marine Fisheries on the top left of the first page. While the rendering pipeline attempted to extract it, the HTML output lacks a proper `` element for this logo, instead displaying a raw text placeholder. Since this is an agency logo on the first page, its absence as an actual image is a critical defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Top left corner of page 1 features the North Carolina Marine Fisheries seal/logo.
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML contains a text placeholder `

[Image: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries featuring a fish over an outline of the state]

` but no actual `` element for the logo.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a continuous ordered list using Roman numerals (I. through X.). In the output, the first part of this list (I through VI on page 1) is correctly extracted as list items. However, starting on page 2, the pipeline breaks the list structure and begins extracting the top-level list items (VII, VIII, IX, and X) as Headings instead of list items. This severely corrupts the nested list hierarchy.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Roman numeral list items such as "VII. PENDER COUNTY", "VIII. NEW HANOVER COUNTY", and "X. GENERAL INFORMATION"
concerns.output_quote: "type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "VII. PENDER COUNTY" (and similar heading extractions for VIII, IX, and X)

concerns.description: Link text pulled over but not hyperlinked
concerns.analysis: Several text phrases that are clickable hyperlinks in the source PDF have been extracted as plain, flat text in the body content, without the expected markdown syntax to linkify them. While the extraction placed link artifact objects at the bottom of the pages containing the URLs, the actual inline body text itself lost its hyperlinked state.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1: "For more information about designated nursery areas visit the NCDMF Interactive Map." (NCDMF Interactive Map is a clickable hyperlink). Page 3: "Go to the latest proclamation for areas..." (latest proclamation is a hyperlink).
concerns.output_quote: "II. Designated primary nursery areas... For more information about designated nursery areas visit the NCDMF Interactive Map."
concerns.location: Page 1, List Item II; Page 3, List Items X.E and X.H

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 4 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=4, examples=['https://ncdenr.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/viewer.html?useEx', 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/marine-fisheries/rules-pr', 'https://files.nc.gov/deq/documents/2023-12/PT-1-2024%20Amend']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (3): State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries
3441 ; Proclamation PT-2-2024
Pg. 2; Proclamation PT-2-2024
Pg. 3
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=3, pages_affected=3/3. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. Designated primary nursery areas in all Internal Coastal"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=1. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 2 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "A. That area between the Highway 58 Bridge and the Bogue Inl"; "A. West Channel - Island Channel - from Intracoastal Waterwa"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=2. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 3 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (3 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ries featuring a fish over an outline of the state"; p2: "...isheries, including their address in Morehead City"; p3: "...rine Fisheries. It provides their physical address"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=3 (truncated=3, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document relies heavily on underlines to emphasize location headers within lists (e.g., 'V. CARTERET COUNTY/ONSLOW COUNTY', 'VI. ONSLOW COUNTY', 'A. West Channel...'). This underline emphasis is completely lost in the JSON output as no structural element handles it. Furthermore, items E and K on page 3 are heavily bolded in the source, but no bold markdown (**) was applied to these paragraphs in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: V. CARTERET COUNTY/ONSLOW COUNTY (underlined text) and K. This proclamation describes areas open to crab... (bolded and underlined text)
concerns.output_quote: "V. CARTERET COUNTY/ONSLOW COUNTY" and "K. This proclamation describes areas open to crab..." (rendered as plain text with no emphasis markdown)
concerns.location: Page 1 List Item V, Page 3 List Item K
